primary_reason: This review organizes and assesses transferable technologies for predictive surveillance, sentinel detection, and population screening.
The scoping review benchmarks outbreak-warning tools, validation practices, implementation feasibility, and evidence gaps.
The study combines modified spatiotemporal clustering with reproduction-number estimation to characterize heterogeneous wildlife transmission.
The comparison concerns laboratory DNA-library preparation kits rather than a transferable epidemiological surveillance or outbreak-analysis method.
The article develops a flexible discrete-time mechanistic epidemic model permitting arbitrary generation-time distributions and accessible computation.
The study constructs and applies a wavelet-based analytical model for norovirus monitoring and seasonal early warning.
The commentary provides substantive guidance on reproduction-number definitions, estimation methods, data choices, and responsible interpretation.
The animal-challenge trials characterize viral pathobiology and clinical signs rather than an eligible epidemiological early-warning method.
The article develops an indoor air-quality sensor network without directly estimating, detecting, or validating infectious-disease events.
The review explains early-warning statistics for infectious-disease transitions and evaluates their expected behavior and limitations.
